steps.label: cause
steps.text: Lactic acid bacteria conduct malolactic fermentation after alcoholic fermentation.

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: The bacteria convert sharper diprotic malic acid into softer monoprotic lactic acid and carbon dioxide; tartaric acid is not converted.

steps.label: effect
steps.text: Titratable acidity decreases, pH rises slightly, and the wine tastes softer and rounder while retaining its tartaric acid.

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Formative causal formulation: Titratable acidity decreases, pH rises slightly, and the wine tastes softer and rounder while retaining its tartaric acid.
